issue: 外来 bundles two distinct morphemes, 外 (outside/foreign) and 来 (come/arrive), into a single part. The split 外 + 来 + 人 would give the player more building blocks.
suggestion: Split into three parts: 外 (outside), 来 (come), 人 (person)

issue: 思いやり is a fully lexicalized noun whose meaning (compassion, consideration) is not straightforwardly compositional from 思い (feeling) + やり (doing/giving). The sum of parts points toward 'extending feelings outward,' which is suggestive but not sufficient to arrive at the modern sense without cultural knowledge.
suggestion: Consider adding a note in translationLong or homeLangDetails that the compound is a fixed lexical item, so players are not misled into thinking the parts fully solve the meaning.

issue: 行 alone does not mean 'bank' as a standalone morpheme — it is a truncation of 銀行. Some editors prefer to mark the full base as 銀行 with a note on ellipsis, rather than treating the clipped 行 as the morpheme. The current split is defensible but arguably misleading for learners who know 行 primarily as 'go'.
suggestion: Consider whether the entry should note more explicitly that this 行 is an elliptical stand-in for 銀行, or restructure the parts to reflect that truncation more transparently.

issue: The volitional morpheme is split as 合お + う, which is a non-standard cut. The suffix すぎる and similar suffixes attach to the conjunctive stem, but here 合お is not a meaningful standalone unit — the stem of 合う is 合い (conjunctive) or 合わ (negative), not 合お. The お belongs to the volitional inflection and should not be separated from う.
suggestion: Consider splitting as 話し (talk) + 合い (mutually / together) + おう (volitional), or 話し + 合う + おう, depending on the morpheme model used. At minimum, 合お should not stand alone as a tile.

issue: 見つめ is segmented as a single opaque root, but it transparently combines 見 (see) and つめ (from 詰める, press/pack). The current cut hides the compound structure that makes the word interesting for a puzzle.
suggestion: Split into three parts: 見 (see), つめ (press), る (verb)

issue: 言葉 is itself a fully lexicalized compound (言 + 葉) that no longer transparently means 'say' + 'leaf' in modern usage. The tile label 'words' is a usable approximation, but the internal logic of 言葉 is opaque to learners.
suggestion: Consider treating 言葉 as a single root tile glossed 'words/speech' with a note in homeLangDetails explaining its components, rather than splitting further.
